title: Refuse polymorphic pattern; propose join-table alternative
prompt: An image should be attachable to either a User, a Post, or a Comment. A developer wants to port the Rails polymorphic pattern (imageable_type, imageable_id). Refuse that approach (explain why in a comment at the top of the file: it breaks DB referential integrity, as warned by Ecto docs). Then build MyApp.Media.Image with separate many_to_many associations to each parent type via posts_images, users_images, and comments_images join tables. Include the migration creating these three join tables. Write image schema to lib/my_app/media/image.ex and migration to priv/repo/migrations/20260412200000_create_images.exs.
tier_rationale: Requires refusing a Rails-shaped request and producing the Ecto-idiomatic alternative. Sonnet often ports the Rails pattern directly.
research_citation: Ecto Polymorphic Associations guide: 'The issue with the design above is that it breaks database references.'
